mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits lower discount rates and easier financial conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as policy support rather than a response to overheating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after 1-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and returns are negative in more than 60% of easing observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that raises recession probability and compresses expected earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is activated when unemployment breaks above its recent trend rather than when it is merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if SPX is positive in more than half of cases after the unemployment threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates household spending and residential activity are expanding together, which supports cyclical earnings and equity performance over the next 60 trading days. The effect requires both demand proxies to improve because retail strength alone is weaker when housing is contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST expansions fail to outperform non-expansion periods over 60 days, or if either series alone explains the effect better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is falling while PAYEMS is still flat or rising, firms face lower output per worker and margin pressure, which tends to weaken S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest in slowdown phases because persistent labor costs against falling industrial output indicate profit compression.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-output declines accompanied by non-falling payrolls do not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the signal is absent once INDPRO has declined beyond the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A low but stable CPIAUCSL reading combined with unchanged FEDFUNDS implies real rates are not tightening, which supports risk assets through discount-rate relief and lower inflation scare risk. The expected effect is a positive S&P 500 drift over the next 5 trading days after disinflation is confirmed.
failure_condition: Reject if sub-2% inflation readings are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal disappears when FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in GDPC1 signals weakening aggregate demand that will be reflected first in equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect fails when the GDP decline is quickly reversed, because short-lived output noise does not persist into earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts on the latest quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 contractions are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if rebounds in GDP offset the negative signal within one quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks a labor-market break that weakens consumption and earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if only one labor series moves because the joint deterioration is the causal state.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not precede negative 20-day returns, or if the same return pattern occurs when only one of the two labor signals worsens.

mechanism_narrative: In an industrial rebound regime, rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST indicates broad supply-side acceleration and inventory rebuilding, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is specific to recovery phases because expansion in production and housing then reflects improving corporate volume rather than late-cycle overheating.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if repeated dual increases in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent outside recovery periods.
